1	# Migration: routing-config deprecation (bead ddx-87fb72c2)
2	
3	This guide covers the breaking change to `agent.routing.*` in
4	`.ddx/config.yaml`. The change ships as part of epic `ddx-fdd3ea36`
5	(endpoint-first routing).
6	
7	## Summary
8	
9	| Field                              | Status         | Action                       |
10	| ---------------------------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------- |
11	| `agent.routing.default_harness`    | **Removed**    | Delete the field             |
12	| `agent.routing.profile_ladders`    | Opt-in only    | Consulted only with `--escalate`        |
13	| `agent.routing.model_overrides`    | Opt-in only    | Consulted only with `--override-model`  |
14	| `agent.harness` (top-level)        | Kept           | Tie-break preference, not a default override |
15	| `agent.routing.profile_priority`   | Already deprecated | Still parsed; warns at load |
16	
17	## `agent.routing.default_harness` → removed
18	
19	The field has been deleted from the schema. DDx now rejects configs that
20	still carry it with a hard error at startup and a pointer back to this
21	guide. Exit code is non-zero.
22	
23	### Before
24	
25	```yaml
26	agent:
27	  harness: claude
28	  routing:
29	    default_harness: agent
30	```
31	
32	### After
33	
34	```yaml
35	agent:
36	  harness: claude
37	```
38	
39	The top-level `agent.harness` field is preserved and remains honored as
40	a tie-break preference during route resolution. It is **not** a silent
41	default override.
42	
43	## `agent.routing.profile_ladders` → opt-in via `--escalate`
44	
45	The default execute path no longer iterates the ladder. A single
46	`ResolveRoute` call drives each attempt; `profile_ladders` is consulted
47	only when the operator explicitly passes `--escalate` to enable
48	tier-ladder escalation semantics.
49	
50	If your config still sets `profile_ladders`, DDx emits a one-time
51	process warning at config-load time:
52	
53	```
54	warning: agent.routing.profile_ladders is opt-in.
55	It is consulted only when --escalate is passed; the default execute
56	path ignores it. See docs/migrations/routing-config.md.
57	```
58	
59	To silence the warning either drop the field, or pass `--escalate` on
60	the runs where you want tier escalation.
61	
62	## `agent.routing.model_overrides` → opt-in via `--override-model`
63	
64	The same treatment as `profile_ladders`. The default execute path
65	ignores `model_overrides`. Pass the new `--override-model` flag to opt
66	into per-tier override consultation:
67	
68	```
69	ddx agent execute-loop --escalate --override-model
70	```
71	
72	A one-time process warning fires at config-load time when the field is
73	set without invocation.
74	
75	## Test commands
76	
77	```bash
78	# Reject a config with the removed field:
79	echo 'version: "1.0"
80	library:
81	  path: ./library
82	  repository:
83	    url: https://example.com/repo
84	    branch: main
85	agent:
86	  routing:
87	    default_harness: claude' > /tmp/bad.yaml
88	DDX_PROJECT_ROOT=/tmp ddx status   # exits non-zero with migration message
89	```
90	
91	## Background
92	
93	Prior to this change, three competing surfaces dictated harness
94	selection: the top-level `agent.harness` field, the routing block's
95	`default_harness`, and the live route-resolution call graph. The
96	endpoint-first redesign (epic ddx-fdd3ea36) collapses harness selection
97	into a single explicit decision per dispatch: route resolution is the
98	source of truth, and `agent.harness` is the operator's tie-break. The
99	ladder + overrides knobs remain available for operators who explicitly
100	want escalation or model substitution, but they are no longer
101	consulted by default.
102

## `agent.routing.profile_ladders` → opt-in via `--escalate`

The default execute path no longer iterates the ladder. A single
`ResolveRoute` call drives each attempt; `profile_ladders` is consulted
only when the operator explicitly passes `--escalate` to enable
tier-ladder escalation semantics.

This applies to **both** dispatch paths:

- `ddx work --local` / `ddx agent execute-loop --local` — the `--escalate`
  flag controls escalation directly on the CLI in-process loop.
- `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-loop` (default, server-submit) — the
  `--escalate` flag is forwarded to the server worker spec
  (`ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.Escalate`), which gates the ladder identically
  inside `cli/internal/server/workers.go`. Bead `ddx-c7081f89` aligned
  the server predicate with the CLI predicate; before that, the server
  worker enabled escalation implicitly whenever no harness/model was
  pinned, which produced "all tiers exhausted" failures on the default
  drain path.

fix(server): align worker escalation with --escalate opt-in [ddx-c7081f89]

The server worker (cli/internal/server/workers.go) enabled tier-ladder
escalation implicitly whenever neither Harness nor Model was pinned. The
CLI's --local path already required --escalate as opt-in (ddx-87fb72c2,
docs/migrations/routing-config.md). Because `ddx work` defaults to
server submission, the user-facing surface still ran the tier loop +
ResolveTierCandidates cooldown probe + AdaptiveMinTier filter on every
dispatch — even though the migration declared the ladder opt-in.

Changes:
- Add Escalate field to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec and a tiny
  escalationEnabledForSpec helper used by runWorker. Predicate now
  matches the CLI:
    spec.Escalate && spec.Harness == "" && spec.Model == ""
- Forward --escalate from the CLI server-submit path through the HTTP
  body, server handler, and GraphQL adapter into the worker spec.
- Pin the contract with workers_escalate_test.go: predicate truth
  table, ResolveProfileLadderCallCount stays at 0 with Escalate=false,
  advances with Escalate=true, and operator pin (Harness or Model)
  always bypasses escalation regardless of the flag.
- Cross-reference the migration doc.

Helper functions ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierCandidates, and
AdaptiveMinTier are intentionally kept; they remain consumed by the
--escalate opt-in path. Their cleanup is reserved for a follow-up bead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Author: Erik LaBianca <erik.labianca@gmail.com>

    fix(server): align worker escalation with --escalate opt-in [ddx-c7081f89]
    
    The server worker (cli/internal/server/workers.go) enabled tier-ladder
    escalation implicitly whenever neither Harness nor Model was pinned. The
    CLI's --local path already required --escalate as opt-in (ddx-87fb72c2,
    docs/migrations/routing-config.md). Because `ddx work` defaults to
    server submission, the user-facing surface still ran the tier loop +
    ResolveTierCandidates cooldown probe + AdaptiveMinTier filter on every
    dispatch — even though the migration declared the ladder opt-in.
    
    Changes:
    - Add Escalate field to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec and a tiny
      escalationEnabledForSpec helper used by runWorker. Predicate now
      matches the CLI:
        spec.Escalate && spec.Harness == "" && spec.Model == ""
    - Forward --escalate from the CLI server-submit path through the HTTP
      body, server handler, and GraphQL adapter into the worker spec.
    - Pin the contract with workers_escalate_test.go: predicate truth
      table, ResolveProfileLadderCallCount stays at 0 with Escalate=false,
      advances with Escalate=true, and operator pin (Harness or Model)
      always bypasses escalation regardless of the flag.
    - Cross-reference the migration doc.
    
    Helper functions ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierCandidates, and
    AdaptiveMinTier are intentionally kept; they remain consumed by the
    --escalate opt-in path. Their cleanup is reserved for a follow-up bead.

  advances with Escalate=true, and operator pin (Harness or Model)
  always bypasses escalation regardless of the flag.

--escalate opt-in path. Their cleanup is reserved for a follow-up bead.

EOF
